gaps: fedcourts query has no way to look up a single case's own corpus row (had to read the corpus db directly to confirm the bare row / predict_excluded state for the scope flag)
no corpus filter for era/Term when docket numbers are absent, so base rates cannot be cut to the case's vintage
helpful: fedcourts stats --resolved-only disposition base rates were the main calibration anchor for this bare-snapshot case
fedcourts paths resolved the event/corpus locations instantly
notes: Snapshot was nearly empty, so the prediction rests on stats base rates plus era classification from the caption. fedcourts query returned mostly unrelated old dockets since this case has no topic/judge/citation metadata to filter on.
tools_used: sqlite3 (read-only, corpus row inspection for the scope flag)
